figures.caption: Fig. 1. Proposed SwitchBraidNet Architecture. The model employs a dual-path temporal braid using deep and fast convolutional kernels to extract multi-scale neural features. The spatial switch utilises a squeeze-and-excitation (SE) block to dynamically weigh electrode importance, followed by a hardware-efficient LogVarLayer that collapses the temporal dimension into stable, low-bit power features suitable for 8-bit QAT optimisation.
Fig. 3. ITR vs. bit-depth across all architectures. SwitchBraidNet maintains the highest throughput and superior robustness at INT8 precision.
Table 1. Performance metrics for single modality paradigms and hybrid BCI systems in sequential and simultaneous modes
Fig. 4. Minimum MI classification accuracy required for the hBCI to exceed the ITR of a pure SSVEP system, as a function of the number of SSVEP targets. SSVEP accuracy is assumed to be perfect.
